diary: Started the day right with my usual swim at the IMA pool—the water was perfect, and I had the lane mostly to myself. Work in the morning was solid; we cleared a backlog of shipments that's been nagging at me all week. Then I caught the USA-Wales match at Big Time Brewery around lunchtime. The place was already packed even though it was only 1:30, everyone buzzing about the World Cup. We didn't play great, but there's something electric about watching it live with strangers who actually care. Got back to the warehouse and powered through the afternoon. Grabbed groceries on the way home in Redmond—nothing fancy, just the usual stuff. The weather was gorgeous all day, one of those June Mondays that reminds you why you live here.
thought_process: The Light Rail closure didn't really affect me since I drove to U-District anyway, but I was glad I'd planned the swim before work—needed that mental reset. When lunchtime hit and the World Cup match was on, I didn't hesitate to head to Big Time. I've been feeling a bit cooped up lately, and honestly the energy at the bar was infectious. It broke up the day nicely. Work-wise, I stayed focused in the morning so I could afford the longer break without guilt. The gorgeous weather put me in a lighter mood overall, and watching sports with a crowd of randoms felt less lonely than eating at my desk would have. By evening I was tired but satisfied—one of those days where small choices added up to feeling pretty good.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
